No Poverty.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports a contribution to eradicating poverty.

Zero Hunger.reason: The course explicitly covers 'Agricultural accounting', directly relevant to sustainable food production and agricultural productivity (Targets 2.3, 2.4).

Good Health and Well-being.reason: Topics like 'Employee benefits' and 'Pension accounting' indirectly support financial risk protection, contributing to well-being (Target 3.8).

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education and develops professional skills like financial reporting, decision-making, and IT analysis for employment (Targets 4.3, 4.4).
evidence: 奠定相關基礎專業知識
溝通能力(Students will acquire professional communication skills.)
問題解決能力(Students will acquire problem solving skills.)

Gender Equality.reason: No evidence found in the course syllabus to support a contribution to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No evidence found in the course syllabus to support a contribution to clean water and sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No evidence found in the course syllabus to support a contribution to affordable and clean energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Course topics like 'Employee benefits' and 'Pension accounting' are central to decent work, protecting labor rights, and promoting productive employment (Targets 8.5, 8.8).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The topic 'Investment property' relates to accounting for assets that can be part of infrastructure, supporting economic development (Target 9.1).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Accounting for 'Employee benefits' and 'Share-based payment' can provide transparency for fiscal and wage policies, indirectly supporting greater equality (Target 10.4).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The study of 'Investment property' is relevant to the financial management of urban development and sustainable human settlement planning (Target 11.3).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: A core competency is to 'identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues', directly supporting the integration of sustainability information into corporate reporting (Target 12.6).
evidence: 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題
Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.

Climate Action.reason: No direct or strong indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports a contribution to climate action.

Life Below Water.reason: No evidence found in the course syllabus to support a contribution to life below water.

Life on Land.reason: The topic 'Agricultural accounting' is relevant for integrating ecosystem values into accounts and promoting sustainable land management (Target 15.9).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Focus on 'international financial reporting standards' and 'ethics' promotes transparent, accountable institutions and helps reduce corruption (Targets 16.5, 16.6).
evidence: 熟悉國際會計準則的應用
最新國際財務報導準則之發展
理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course's foundation in 'international financial reporting standards' exemplifies global partnerships and policy coherence for macroeconomic stability (Targets 17.13, 17.16).
evidence: 涵蓋最新國際財務報導準則之發展
學習國際會計準則基礎的會計處理及財務報導原則
